original_abstract: Abstract While originally designed for natural language processing tasks, the self-attention mechanism has recently taken various computer vision areas by storm. However, the 2D nature of images brings three challenges for applying self-attention in computer vision: (1) treating images as 1D sequences neglects their 2D structures; (2) the quadratic complexity is too expensive for high-resolution images; (3) it only captures spatial adaptability but ignores channel adaptability. In this paper, we propose a novel linear attention named large kernel attention (LKA) to enable self-adaptive and long-range correlations in self-attention while avoiding its shortcomings. Furthermore, we present a neural network based on LKA, namely Visual Attention Network (VAN). While extremely simple, VAN achieves comparable results with similar size convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) in various tasks, including image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, panoptic segmentation, pose estimation, etc. For example, VAN-B6 achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet benchmark, and sets new state-of-the-art performance (58.2 PQ) for panoptic segmentation. Besides, VAN-B2 surpasses Swin-T 4 mIoU (50.1 vs. 46.1) for semantic segmentation on ADE20K benchmark, 2.6 AP (48.8 vs. 46.2) for object detection on COCO dataset. It provides a novel method and a simple yet strong baseline for the community. The code is available at https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network .
humanized_abstract: Original Abstract The self-attention mechanism, which was proposed for natural language processing, has gradually become a hot research direction in the computer vision field in recent years. However, for images, the application of self-attention is also restricted by three aspects: 1) taking images as 1D sequences will ignore the 2D information of images; 2) the high computational complexity, which is proportional to the square of the length of the sequence, will lead to severe performance bottleneck in the case of large images; 3) the self-attention mechanism can only show the spatial adaptability, while lacking the channel adaptability. In this paper, we propose a new linear attention called large kernel attention (LKA), and based on it, design a new network structure called Visual Attention Network (VAN). To our surprise, the VAN is very simple, but experimental results show that the models of the same scale even exceed the performance of many popular CNNs and ViTs in the tasks of image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, panoptic segmentation, and keypoint estimation, such as achieving 87.8% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with VAN-B6, and even achieving the state-of-the-art result of 58.2 PQ on panoptic segmentation, and exceeding Swin-T 4mIoU by 4 on ADE20K semantic segmentation, and exceeding the AP of Swin-T on COCO object detection by 2.6, etc. The code is available at https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network .

document.output: Original Abstract The self-attention mechanism, which was proposed for natural language processing, has gradually become a hot research direction in the computer vision field in recent years. However, for images, the application of self-attention is also restricted by three aspects: 1) taking images as 1D sequences will ignore the 2D information of images; 2) the high computational complexity, which is proportional to the square of the length of the sequence, will lead to severe performance bottleneck in the case of large images; 3) the self-attention mechanism can only show the spatial adaptability, while lacking the channel adaptability. In this paper, we propose a new linear attention called large kernel attention (LKA), and based on it, design a new network structure called Visual Attention Network (VAN). To our surprise, the VAN is very simple, but experimental results show that the models of the same scale even exceed the performance of many popular CNNs and ViTs in the tasks of image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, panoptic segmentation, and keypoint estimation, such as achieving 87.8% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with VAN-B6, and even achieving the state-of-the-art result of 58.2 PQ on panoptic segmentation, and exceeding Swin-T 4mIoU by 4 on ADE20K semantic segmentation, and exceeding the AP of Swin-T on COCO object detection by 2.6, etc. The code is available at https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network .
document.input: Abstract While originally designed for natural language processing tasks, the self-attention mechanism has recently taken various computer vision areas by storm. However, the 2D nature of images brings three challenges for applying self-attention in computer vision: (1) treating images as 1D sequences neglects their 2D structures; (2) the quadratic complexity is too expensive for high-resolution images; (3) it only captures spatial adaptability but ignores channel adaptability. In this paper, we propose a novel linear attention named large kernel attention (LKA) to enable self-adaptive and long-range correlations in self-attention while avoiding its shortcomings. Furthermore, we present a neural network based on LKA, namely Visual Attention Network (VAN). While extremely simple, VAN achieves comparable results with similar size convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) in various tasks, including image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, panoptic segmentation, pose estimation, etc. For example, VAN-B6 achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet benchmark, and sets new state-of-the-art performance (58.2 PQ) for panoptic segmentation. Besides, VAN-B2 surpasses Swin-T 4 mIoU (50.1 vs. 46.1) for semantic segmentation on ADE20K benchmark, 2.6 AP (48.8 vs. 46.2) for object detection on COCO dataset. It provides a novel method and a simple yet strong baseline for the community. The code is available at https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network .
